concerns.analysis:
The source document contains an agency seal on page 1 and a signature block on page 3. In the HTML output, the page 1 seal is missing and instead rendered only as text inside brackets (`

[Image: ...]

`). Additionally, the image manifest indicates that the signature image on page 3 has a `valid: false` status with an error, meaning the base64 data is corrupt and will fail to render.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Seal of the North Carolina Marine Fisheries on Page 1; signature image on Page 3.
concerns.output_quote:
[Image: Seal of the North Carolina Marine Fisheries... ]

and an tag for the signature where the manifest reports `"valid": false, "error": "No module named 'PIL'"`

concerns.analysis: The source document contains a primary list utilizing Roman numerals (I, II, III, IV) with nested sub-lists using capital letters (A, B, C). On page 1, the pipeline correctly groups items I, II, and III as an ordered list with proper nesting for their sub-items. However, on page 2, the final parent item 'IV. GENERAL INFORMATION' is incorrectly extracted as a level-2 heading instead of a list item. This breaks the main list sequence and causes its nested sub-items (A-J on page 2, K-M on page 3) t
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
IV. GENERAL INFORMATION
A. This proclamation is issued under the authority...
concerns.output_quote: {"type": "heading", "level": 2, "text": "IV. GENERAL INFORMATION"}, {"type": "list", "list_type": "ordered", "items": [{"text": "A. This proclamation is issued under the authority...

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 3 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=3, examples=['https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-50/part-648/subpart-i', 'https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-50/part-648/subpart-i', 'http://www.ncmarinefisheries.net/']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (1): State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries
3441
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

The source document heavily uses underlining and bolding for emphasis, particularly on restriction statements and list headers. This emphasis is lost entirely in several areas of the output. For example, 'FISH TRAPS/POTS:' and 'TRAWLS:' are bolded in the source but are extracted as plain text. Furthermore, item 'M' on page 3 is fully bolded and underlined in the source but appears as completely unformatted plain text in the output.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: M. This proclamation supersedes Proclamation FF-4-2023, dated December 16, 2022. It increases the offload limit for trawls in the Atlantic Ocean commercial black sea bass fishery north of Cape Hatteras...
concerns.output_quote: "text": "M. This proclamation supersedes Proclamation FF-4-2023, dated December 16, 2022. It increases the offload limit for trawls in the Atlantic Ocean commercial black sea bass fishery north of Cape Hatteras..."

concerns.description: 2 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-50/part-; www.ncmarinefisheries.net.
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=2. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links.
